etymology.description: Ś§ź (abbreviated phonetic) + ŚĽĺ (semantic 'hands') -> indicates authority with eye and stick, hands imply 'numerous' -> final meaning enriched with 'brilliant'
etymology.interpretation: Ś•ź combines a phonetic component that suggests authority and prominence with a semantic component depicting hands, implying abundance. The combination led to an associative meaning of eminence and copiousness, sometimes extended to brilliance.
etymology.simplification: Ś•ź was simplified to Ś•ā, combining and stylizing components to preserve phonetic clarity.
